text:
Once upon a quiet spring afternoon, in a quaint seaside town named Whiskersburgh, nestled among lavender hills, there lived a wise old cat named Orion. Orion was white with turquoise eyes and slept curled up atop an old-fashioned rocking stool in his backyard.

Orion was loved by everyone—in fact, maybe too much. Because every bird wanted to sing with him after dusk. Every cloud wanted to share its rainbow. And every squirrel wanted to eat his tuna.

One day, a new moon dawned, casting silvery light over everything. Curious about what could change mysteriously, Orion went out late to watch.

Then, he saw it. A shadow danced across the sky—a crescent moon, swift as thought.

That night, Orion decided to experiment. He sang—old poems about stars twinkling. He murmured secrets to the wind. Then, he hummed tales of bravery.

Next morning, he watched quietly as things began to change. The clouds parted just enough for sunlight
